name: Pre-excited AF — WPW + Atrial Fibrillation
identifyBy: Irregular wide-complex tachycardia at very high rate (>200 bpm)
Wide bizarre QRS complexes (delta wave + accessory pathway conduction)
Rate can exceed 300 bpm — ventricular fibrillation risk
ECG looks chaotic — often misidentified as VF or VT
management: If haemodynamically unstable: immediate synchronised DC cardioversion
AVOID AV nodal blocking drugs: Adenosine CONTRAINDICATED, Verapamil CONTRAINDICATED, Digoxin CONTRAINDICATED
These drugs block the AV node, forcing all conduction down the accessory pathway at maximum rate — can precipitate VF
Procainamide (preferred) or Flecainide may slow accessory pathway conduction
If deteriorates to VF: defibrillate 200J unsynchronised
pearls: The most dangerous WPW presentation — AV nodal drugs are potentially fatal
If in doubt between this and VF: defibrillate — safe for both
Rate >250 bpm in irregular wide complex = pre-excited AF until proven otherwise
The irregularity distinguishes pre-excited AF from VT (VT is usually regular)
After conversion: definitive treatment is accessory pathway ablation
differentials: Polymorphic VT (different morphology, usually no delta waves)
AF with aberrant conduction (LBBB/RBBB — but rate <200 bpm usually)
